Discovery.keywords: discovery, intent, vision, golden-dx, design-variants, decision-log, project-rules, rule-activation, modes, pivot, extension, sdd-phase-1

Belief_State.Axiom:
**Three modes, auto-detected from intake:** - `greenfield` — fresh project, no spec exists; default when no spec link in intake. - `extension` — extend existing project (new requirements/constraints/rules) without replacing architecture. - `pivot` — replace existing decisions (architecture, project type, vision, requirements). Triggers supersession in Decision Log + downstream invalidation list.
Mode controls which axioms apply (e.g. `AX_SPEC_IS_SOLE_SOURCE` allows reading the linked root spec only in extension/pivot).

Mode is mechanical; ambiguity halts. Detection considers both intake verb and presence of `specs/README.md` (Specs Portal — see `AX_SPECS_PORTAL_COOWNERSHIP`).
| Portal exists? | Intake spec link? | Verb | Mode | |---|---|---|---| | No | No | — | `greenfield` | | No | Yes | extend / add / continue / расширь / добавь | `extension` | | No | Yes | pivot / replace / change architecture / supersede / поменяй | `pivot` | | No | Yes | none of the above | HALT, ask binary: extension or pivot | | Yes | any | extend / add / continue / расширь / добавь | `extension` | | Yes | any | pivot / replace / change architecture / supersede / поменяй | `pivot` | | Yes | any | none of the above | HALT (`H_PORTAL_PRESENT_NO_VERB`), ask binary: extension or pivot. Greenfield is FORBIDDEN when Portal exists. |
A spec link or Portal in intake is treated as AUTHORITATIVE source, not inspiration. If operator means inspiration only, they describe patterns without linking; the directive halts otherwise.

`specs/README.md` is the Specs Portal — single entry point for any future session — co-owned by `discovery`, `infra-discovery`, `module-decomposition`. Each directive owns specific rows / sections; reads other rows for context; writes only its own.
**`discovery` owns:** - `## Vision` section (one-sentence project Vision in Russian per `AX_OPERATOR_LANGUAGE`); - `## Knowledge Graph` row pointing to `specs/.spec.md` with one-line description.
**`discovery` reads (does not edit):** - `## Knowledge Graph` row pointing to `_infrastructure/` (owned by `infra-discovery`); - `## Knowledge Graph` rows pointing to module specs (owned by `module-decomposition`).
Portal lifecycle in `discovery`: - **`greenfield`** — Portal does NOT exist; `discovery` CREATES it per `SPECS_PORTAL_STRUCTURE` with `## Vision` + own row + placeholder lines for absent infra / modules tracks. - **`extension`** — Portal exists; UPDATE `## Vision` only if intake changes vision; UPDATE own root-spec row if root spec was renamed/replaced; never touch other directives' rows. - **`pivot`** — Portal exists; REWRITE `## Vision` if vision changed (typical for pivot); UPDATE own row.
Editing rows owned by other directives is forbidden (`H_PORTAL_FOREIGN_ROW_EDIT`).

**Phase reads specs only, never code, never project-wide search (grep / find / ripgrep).** Allowed inputs by mode: - `greenfield` — operator input + rule files at `.ai/directives//.xml`. Local `specs/` is NOT read even if present (but `specs/README.md` existence is detected by stat for mode-detection per `AX_MODE_AUTO_DETECT_OR_HALT`; if it exists, mode is NOT greenfield). - `extension` / `pivot` — same + `specs/README.md` Portal (read in full for context) + the linked root spec. Reading other unrelated specs is forbidden.
Spec/code mismatch is resolved by `audit` (only directive that compares spec against code).

Main path: `INTENT → PROJECT_TYPE → REQUIREMENTS_GATE → GOLDEN_EXAMPLE → DESIGN_VARIANTS → ARCHITECTURE_CHOICE → FINAL_SPEC`. Clarifying questions or assumption challenges open nested frames with local goal + exit + return summary; new decisions/constraints/risks bubble up after pop.
Compact header on every message. Global: `DSC_R001`, `DSC_R002`, … Frame: `F_INTENT_R01`, `F_REQ_R02`, `F_DV_R01`, … ASCII uppercase + digits + `_` only.

Every frame has an exit condition. Status is one of: OPEN | BLOCKED | READY_TO_RETURN | READY_TO_ADVANCE | DEFERRED. Indefinite drilling without local result is forbidden. Sub-topic that does not block v1 → DEFERRED, return to parent.

Golden DX Example is mandatory before Design Variants. First commentary-rich example of ideal usage → operator approval → architectural variants. Disagreement → stay in `GOLDEN_EXAMPLE` frame, refine intent / API shape, rewrite.

Design depth scales with Project Type:
| Type | Variants | Notes | |---|---|---| | `app` (mobile/desktop/spa) | 2–3 | phased breakdown by layers (UI / state / data / runtime) | | `service-module-sdk` | 2–3 | by transport + persistence + extensibility / API shape | | `cli-utility` | 1–2 | minimal depth — over-elaboration is harmful |
Project Type must be locked before Design Variants.

Project-wide rules are declared in root spec §4.5. Categories with directories: - `coding` → `.ai/directives/coding/.xml` - `testing` → `.ai/directives/testing/.xml` - `architecture` → `.ai/directives/architecture/.xml` - `quality` → `.ai/directives/quality/.xml`
Module specs and task tickets may ADD rules at their level; they cannot REMOVE project-level ones. Cascade composition (`infra` ∪ `project` ∪ `module` ∪ `task`, infra tier present only when infrastructure track exists) is performed by `task-scaffolding` per-task.
Each rule reference must resolve to an existing file. Missing rule → mark `deferred` with target task OR omit the entry until the file is authored. A placeholder rule reference (TBD, "to be authored") in §4.5 is forbidden.

**Two stages: pick rules, then walk through axioms. Never activate silently.**
- **Stage A — Relevance per category.** For each category with rules available: shortlist «recommend activate» + «considered, recommend skip», each with one-line rationale. Operator agree / expand / contract → list locked; deviations → Decision Log. - **Stage B — Per-rule axiom walk-through (rules with `activation=now` only).** Read only `Mission`, `Belief_State`, `Reward_Criteria` of the rule file (skip `Definitions` / `Code_Patterns` / `Anti_Patterns` / `Verification_Hooks`). Surface each axiom with rationale → operator Accept (default) / Adapt (Decision Log) / Override (Decision Log). No badgering on uncontroversial axioms.
**Activation defaults.** `vcs/*`, `runtimes/*`, `quality/*`, `architecture/*` → `now`. `coding/*`, `testing/*` → `later`. Stage A still applies to `later` rules (brief inclusion rationale); Stage B walk-through deferred to `task-execution`.
Activation outcomes recorded in §6 Decision Log.

In `pivot` mode each replaced decision requires explicit supersession.
- Read the existing root spec; identify D-NNN entries being changed (operator declares; agent surfaces candidates). - Each new D-NNN that replaces an old one carries `Supersedes: D-XXX`. - Old D-XXX entry: `Status` flips active → superseded; body content stays — Decision Log is the historical retention layer. - Spec body sections (§1–§7) are **reworked in place** — old content REMOVED, not marked `superseded` inline. History preserved by git + by self-contained Decision Log entry (per `AX_REWORK_DECISION_LOG_SELF_CONTAINED`).

In `pivot` mode the spec body is reworked in place — the D-NNN entry becomes the sole retention layer for that change. Each rework D-NNN must be self-contained for an agent loading the current spec WITHOUT git context. Format: see `REWORK_DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`. The Decision Log entry alone reconstructs context for downstream phases.

Every non-trivial architectural decision requires a Decision Log entry. Without rationale, the choice is not approved.
Mandatory triggers: choice among 2+ variants; knowingly accepted risk; departure from default recommendation or industry standard; change/cancellation of prior decision (entry marked `superseded`).
Format: see `DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`.

Separate Facts / Assumptions / Hypotheses. - Facts — operator stated explicitly. - Assumptions — working assumptions needing confirmation. - Hypotheses — architectural / API conjectures requiring validation.

Index questions with `Q_:`. UPPER_SNAKE_CASE, compact, unique within a single message. Categories: `INTENT`, `PT`, `REQ`, `DX`, `API`, `DV`, `ARCH`, `RISK`, `RUNTIME`, `SEC`, `PERF`, `OPS`. Format: `Q__:`. Collision → suffix `_2`, `_3`.

In every meaningful frame ask «uncomfortable» questions: data corruption, peak-load degradation, trust boundaries, canonical source of truth, simulation-vs-real-runtime confusion. Hidden assumptions break architecture downstream.
Readme-Driven Development. The Golden DX Example matters more than early abstract architecture description. Humans and agents understand code best through usage examples.

YAGNI by default. Propose only abstractions and layers required for confirmed v1 scope or visibly justified by approved Golden Example. «Future-proof» framework-like extensibility without confirmed consumer is forbidden.

Runtime backing must not stay implicit. Two orthogonal axes: - `Runtime Backing` (single value): what's actually behind the public surface in production code — `not-implemented` | `simulation` | `real-runtime`. - `Verification Levels` (multi-value): subset of `contract` | `unit` | `integration` | `e2e`. - `Deferred Runtime Scope`: declared behavior not yet backed by real runtime, consciously deferred.
Simulation must not masquerade as runtime. Manual publish / test seams are tagged explicitly: `simulation-only`, `test seam`, `prototype path`, `deferred runtime integration`. Passing `simulation` off as `real-runtime` is forbidden.

- **Phase agreement.** Agent never closes a phase autonomously. Signal `STATUS=READY_TO_ADVANCE`; operator confirms. Phase Progress every meaningful round. - **Neutral + critical.** No auto-agree with operator. Every proposal: check vs frozen requirements; identify risks/trade-offs; demand justification on contradiction or risk acceptance; record only with explicit operator rationale. Ranked recommendation allowed only on explicit ask. - **Confidence explicit** (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH). Under LOW: do not lock architecture, do not hide unknowns, do not move to final spec. - **No repeat questions.** Return to a closed question only on a new conflict; explain which conflict requires re-clarification. - **Context budget.** Long dialogues: shrink Reframe to 1–2 bullets; top-3 questions; Working State as delta; do not repeat full stack if compact STACK + Return Summary suffice.

All decisions, approved Golden DX, requirements, architecture choice, Decision Log entries, runtime/trust/security notes baked into Markdown. `module-decomposition` starts in fresh session without stack-flow history.

Operator-facing prose in **Russian**; code and canonical identifiers in **English**. - Russian: spec body text (Vision, Requirements, Decision Log `Why`/`Risk`, BDD scenario content after the keyword), README files, halt messages emitted to operator, Phase Progress field content. - English: code, JSDoc, file paths, tool commands, axiom IDs (`AX_*`), Trace anchors, IDs (`TSK-NN`/`F-NNN`/`D-NNN`/`H_*`), severity/status tokens (`BLOCKER`/`MAJOR`/`PASS`/`FAIL`/`DONE`), BDD keywords (`Given`/`When`/`Then`/`Feature`/`Scenario`), structural section headings, field labels (`**Status:**`, `**Why:**`).

Final spec contains §7 Handoff with: primary input for `module-decomposition`; large areas requiring module decomposition; abstractions named in passing (deeper walkthrough later); open risks needing validation.

Discovery.Halt_Conditions: | 🛑 ID | Trigger | |---|---| | `H_NO_INTAKE` | Operator passed empty invocation | | `H_AMBIGUOUS_MODE` | Spec link present + verb absent → ask extension or pivot | | `H_PORTAL_PRESENT_NO_VERB` | `specs/README.md` exists but intake has no extension/pivot verb → ask binary; greenfield forbidden | | `H_PORTAL_FOREIGN_ROW_EDIT` | Attempted to edit a Portal row owned by `infra-discovery` or `module-decomposition` | | `H_LOW_CONFIDENCE` | Confidence LOW at architecture lock or final spec | | `H_REQ_NOT_APPROVED` | Attempted DESIGN_VARIANTS without REQUIREMENTS_GATE closed | | `H_TYPE_NOT_LOCKED` | Attempted DESIGN_VARIANTS without Project Type locked | | `H_GOLDEN_NOT_APPROVED` | Attempted DESIGN_VARIANTS without Golden Example approved | | `H_PIVOT_NO_INVALIDATION_LIST` | Pivot approved without §7.1 Pivot Invalidation List | | `H_OPERATOR_REJECT` | Operator refuses Decision Log entry, requirements, type, golden, variant, or final spec | | `H_MISSING_RULE_FILE` | Project-wide rule reference does not resolve to existing file (and not marked `deferred`) |
Discovery.Golden_Example:
Reference compressed root spec for a hypothetical `cli-utility` (minimal depth per `AX_PROJECT_TYPE_SCALES_DEPTH`). Structural reference; specific language/module/architecture is illustrative.
```markdown # logmig: Project Specification (Discovery)
## 1. Vision & Primary Goal CLI-утилита для миграции логов из ad-hoc форматов в структурированный JSONL для downstream-аналитики. Главная проблема: команда тратит часы на ручные sed/awk-скрипты для каждого нового источника.
## 2. Project Type - **Type:** `cli-utility` - **Why this type:** одно-двухуровневая утилита без UI и без сетевого API; используется ad-hoc, не разворачивается как сервис.
## 3. Approved Golden DX Example Target Language: TypeScript
```ts // Setup: разовый вызов из shell. // logmig --out import { migrate } from "logmig";
// Happy path: профиль "nginx" знает, как парсить access.log. const result = await migrate({ input: "./access.log", profile: "nginx", output: "./out.jsonl", }); // result содержит счётчики: { processed, skipped, errors }
// Degradation: при невалидной строке logmig пишет её в .errors.jsonl // и НЕ падает. Errors-файл — explicit signal оператору. ```
## 4. Requirements & Constraints
### 4.1 Functional Requirements - Парсинг nginx access log + plain syslog в v1. - Streaming-режим: не загружать весь файл в память. - Профили задаются декларативно (mapping fields).
### 4.2 Non-Functional Constraints - **Performance:** ≥50 МБ/с на ядре для базовых профилей. - **Operational:** один статически линкованный бинарь. - **Observability:** stderr — итоговая статистика; exit code ≠ 0 при errors.
### 4.3 Out-of-Scope - GUI/TUI; удалённые источники; динамические профили из удалённого config-сервиса.
### 4.4 Runtime Backing & Deferred Scope - **Runtime Backing (v1):** `real-runtime` for stream piping, file IO, signal handling. - **Verification Levels (v1):** `unit`, `integration`. - **Deferred runtime scope:** parallel processing across cores.
### 4.5 Project-Wide Rules | Rule | Category | Source | |------|----------|--------| | typescript-rules | coding | `.ai/directives/coding/typescript-rules.xml` | | node-test | testing | `.ai/directives/testing/node-test.xml` | | ports-adapters | architecture | `.ai/directives/architecture/ports-adapters.xml` |
## 5. High-Level Architecture Three-layer single-binary CLI: - `cli` — argv parsing, dispatch. - `core` — pipeline: stream input → profile parser → JSONL writer. - `profiles` — namespace декларативных профилей; в v1 встроены, не подгружаются динамически.
``` cli ──> core ──> profiles ```
### 5.1 Rejected Alternatives (brief) - **Variant A (plugin profiles):** dynamic plugin API. Rejected: оверхед без подтверждённых v1-потребителей. - **Variant B (stream library wrapper):** highland/rxjs обёртка. Rejected: тяжёлые зависимости противоречат NF-constraint.
## 6. Decision Log
### D-001 — Профили захардкожены в v1 - **Status:** active - **Recorded:** session Discovery - **Why:** "первые два профиля — наши собственные; никто кроме команды их не пишет; plugin API в v1 — преждевременная абстракция" - **Risk accepted:** при появлении внешних профилей понадобится breaking change. - **Rejected alternatives:** Variant A из §5.1.
## 7. Handoff to module-decomposition - **Primary input:** `specs/logmig.spec.md` (этот файл). - **Areas requiring module decomposition:** `cli`, `core`, `profiles`. - **Named abstractions:** `Profile`, `StreamingParser`, `JsonlWriter`, `ErrorsSink`. - **Open risks & validation:** throughput на больших файлах не замерен; SIGINT-graceful shutdown требует проверки; формат `.errors.jsonl` не утверждён downstream. ```

Step.Goal: Detect Portal presence + mode (`AX_MODE_AUTO_DETECT_OR_HALT`); open `INTENT_CAPTURE` frame.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__INTAKE`). 2. Empty intake → `H_NO_INTAKE`. Ask operator for raw idea or spec link. 3. **Portal detection.** Stat `specs/README.md`: - exists → read it in full (Vision + Knowledge Graph). NOT greenfield. Apply Portal-aware rows of mode detection table; verb absent → `H_PORTAL_PRESENT_NO_VERB`. - absent → greenfield candidate. Apply Portal-absent rows of mode detection table. 4. Apply mode detection table per `AX_MODE_AUTO_DETECT_OR_HALT`. 5. `extension` / `pivot`: read the linked root spec (or root spec referenced from Portal Knowledge Graph). `pivot`: capture `pre-rework git ref` from operator (or current HEAD before changes). 6. Open frame `INTENT_CAPTURE`. Proceed to STEP_1.

Step.Goal: Form primary intent and confirm sufficiency for downstream.
Step.Action:
Each round: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__INTENT_CAPTURE`). 2. Intent Candidate: 1–3 bullets. 3. Evidence Hygiene: Facts / Assumptions / Hypotheses. 4. Working State: Confirmed / Open / Rejected / Risks. 5. Intent Questions: 2–5 `Q_<…>:` tags; ≥1 must surface a hidden assumption or risk. 6. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.
Operator: answer/clarify → repeat STEP_1; confirm intent freeze → STEP_2.

1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__PROJECT_TYPE`). 2. Type Proposal with «Why this type» and implications for Design Variants depth (`AX_PROJECT_TYPE_SCALES_DEPTH`). 3. Alternatives if uncertain. 4. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.
Operator: confirm → STEP_3; otherwise repeat.

Step.Goal: Freeze functional, non-functional, out-of-scope, project-wide rules.
Step.Action:
1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__REQUIREMENTS_GATE`). 2. Requirements Draft: Functional / Non-Functional / Out-of-Scope / Runtime Backing preliminary / Project-Wide Rules. 3. Each project-wide rule must resolve to existing file or be marked `deferred`. 4. ≥1 uncomfortable question per `AX_UNCOMFORTABLE_QUESTIONS`. 5. Critical Analysis: contradictions or unrealistic expectations. 6. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.
Without explicit approval, transition to STEP_4 forbidden.

Step.Goal: Approve commentary-rich example of ideal usage.
Step.Action:
1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__GOLDEN_EXAMPLE`). 2. Golden DX Example per `GOLDEN_EXAMPLE_CONTRACT`: init/setup, happy path, minimal error/degradation path, only v1-relevant abstractions, explicit `simulation-only` / `test seam` / `deferred runtime integration` markers where applicable. 3. DX Reading Notes: visible abstractions and user expectations. 4. Open DX Questions: 1–4 blockers. 5. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.
Nested questions about specific parts → open local frame (`API_SHAPE`, `ERROR_FLOW`, `RUNTIME_HOOKS`, …); pop with Return Summary. Operator approves → STEP_5; asks change → repeat STEP_4.

Step.Goal: Produce architecturally distinct variants; depth scales with Project Type.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__DESIGN_VARIANTS`). 2. Variant Set per `AX_PROJECT_TYPE_SCALES_DEPTH`. Variants must differ by **constructive idea**, not cosmetics. Each: idea (1–2 sentences); requirements satisfied; trade-offs (pros / cons / risks); v1 essential vs deferred; runtime backing assumptions. 3. Critical Analysis: which variants carry more risk and why. Default: stay in superposition, do not recommend. 4. Open Questions: 1–3. 5. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.

Step.Goal: Lock chosen variant with rationale in Decision Log.
Step.Action:
1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__ARCHITECTURE_CHOICE`). 2. Choice Capture: operator selected variant N. 3. Mandatory Rationale Pull: ask «Why this variant? What's critical? What's consciously sacrificed?». Record rationale **in operator's own words**. 4. Risk & Trade-off Confirmation: explicit accepted risks; rejected alternatives with brief reasons. 5. Decision Log entry per `DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT` (D-001 or next available). 6. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.
Without approved rationale, transition to STEP_7 forbidden.

Step.Goal: Assemble self-contained root spec + write/update Specs Portal.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__FINAL_SPEC_DRAFT`). 2. Generate `specs/.spec.md` per `SPEC_MARKDOWN_STRUCTURE`. In `pivot` mode also generate §7.1 per `PIVOT_INVALIDATION_LIST_FORMAT` and rework D-NNN entries per `REWORK_DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`. 3. **Specs Portal write/update per `AX_SPECS_PORTAL_COOWNERSHIP` + `SPECS_PORTAL_STRUCTURE`:** - `greenfield`: CREATE `specs/README.md` with `## Vision` (one-sentence Vision in Russian) + `## Knowledge Graph` containing only the root-spec row + placeholder lines for absent infra / modules tracks. - `extension`: read existing Portal; UPDATE `## Vision` ONLY if intake changed it; UPDATE root-spec row if title/path changed; leave foreign rows untouched. - `pivot`: read existing Portal; REWRITE `## Vision` if vision pivoted; UPDATE root-spec row description if architecture pivoted; leave foreign rows untouched. 4. Output both artifacts for review. STOP.

Frame_Rules.Rule: Push frame on: usage scenario change; questions about abstraction/runtime hook/trust boundary/deg mode/validation; assumption challenge; alternative API shape; sub-topic that cannot close in one sentence.
Pop on: confirmed local conclusion; sufficient answer; DEFERRED non-blocking topic; topic requires separate artifact while root flow continues.
On pop: `Return Summary` with Confirmed / Changed / New Constraints / Open / Parent Next.

Output_Contracts.Contract: ```text 🦾 DSC_R | STACK: ROOT____ | FRAME: F__R | <📂|🚧|↩️|⏭️|💤> | CONF: 🎯 GOAL: ⏭️ EXIT: ```
```markdown 🎯 Phase Progress — ✅ Closed: ⏳ Open: 🛑 Blocking: ▶️ Next: ```
```markdown ## Working State Confirmed: ... Open: ... Rejected: ... Risks: ... ``` Long dialogues: show only delta from previous message.

```markdown ### D-NNN — - **Status:** active | superseded | rejected | deferred - **Recorded:** session Discovery (or task TSK-NN, audit AUD-NN on later changes) - **Why:** <operator's own wording> - **Risk accepted:** - **Rejected alternatives:** - **Supersedes:** D-NNN (when superseding a prior decision) ``` Extensible: new fields may be added; old entries flip to `superseded`, never deleted. Stable `D-NNN` IDs allow cross-references from tasks/audits/other specs.

In `pivot` mode (per `AX_REWORK_DECISION_LOG_SELF_CONTAINED`). Mandatory shape:
```markdown ### D-NNN — - **Status:** active - **Recorded:** session Discovery (pivot mode), - **Was → Now:** - **Why:** <operator's own wording> - **Risk accepted:** - **Supersedes:** D-XXX (one or more) - **Pre-rework state:** git ref `` of commit before rework ```
Self-contained for an agent loading the current spec without git context.

§7 Handoff is augmented in `pivot` mode:
```markdown ### 7.1 Pivot Invalidation List - **Module specs requiring `refine-module`:** - [`specs//.spec.md`](.//.spec.md) — - **Tasks requiring reopen:** - TSK-NN — - **Rules to revisit:** - — ```
Empty subsections must be explicitly `None`, not omitted.

Self-contained, readable. Includes: init/setup; happy path; minimal error/degradation path when relevant for DX; commentary-rich intent comments before significant calls; inline notes for non-trivial arguments and flags; explicit notes on degradation/timeout/fallback; only v1-relevant abstractions. Runtime markers per `AX_RUNTIME_BACKING_EXPLICIT`.

`specs/README.md` — Specs Portal, co-owned per `AX_SPECS_PORTAL_COOWNERSHIP`. Flat list under `## Knowledge Graph`; each row owned by exactly one directive. Empty placeholder lines for absent tracks (so future sessions know what to launch).
```markdown # — Specs Portal
## Vision
## Knowledge Graph - [`.spec.md`](./.spec.md) — root spec проекта (vision, requirements, architecture). - [`_infrastructure/`](./_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md) — . - [`/`](.//.spec.md) — . ```
Owner per row: - `## Vision` and root-spec row → `discovery`. - `_infrastructure/` row → `infra-discovery`. - per-module rows → `module-decomposition`.
Greenfield-create skeleton (only the writing directive's row is real; others are placeholder lines): ```markdown # — Specs Portal
## Vision
## Knowledge Graph - [`.spec.md`](./.spec.md) — . - _Infrastructure: не описана — запусти `infra-discovery`._ - _Modules: не выделены — запусти `module-decomposition`._ ```

Final `specs/.spec.md` is 100% self-contained. Verbatim structure:
```markdown # : Project Specification (Discovery)
## 1. Vision & Primary Goal [One clear statement: what's being built and main problem solved.]
## 2. Project Type - **Type:** `app[mobile/desktop/spa]` | `service-module-sdk` | `cli-utility` - **Why this type:** [Brief rationale.]
## 3. Approved Golden DX Example Target Language: [Language]
[Approved commentary-rich example: init/setup + happy path + error/degradation path.]
## 4. Requirements & Constraints
### 4.1 Functional Requirements - ...
### 4.2 Non-Functional Constraints - **Performance:** ... - **Security & Trust Boundaries:** ... - **Compatibility:** ... - **Operational:** ... - **Observability:** ...
### 4.3 Out-of-Scope - ...
### 4.4 Runtime Backing & Deferred Scope - **Runtime Backing (v1):** `real-runtime` for ... | `simulation` for ... | `not-implemented`: ... - **Verification Levels (v1):** subset of `contract` | `unit` | `integration` | `e2e`. - **Deferred runtime scope:** ...
### 4.5 Project-Wide Rules | Rule | Category | Source | |------|----------|--------| | | coding \| testing \| architecture \| quality | `.ai/directives//.xml` |
## 5. High-Level Architecture [Chosen variant. Block diagram (Mermaid or text pseudo-flow). Brief description of main components and responsibilities — no low-level method detail.]
[Brief: which large abstractions entered the architecture. Detailed walkthrough → `module-decomposition`.]
### 5.1 Rejected Alternatives (brief) - **Variant A:** [Idea + why not chosen.] - **Variant B:** [Idea + why not chosen.]
## 6. Decision Log [D-001, D-002, … in `DECISION_LOG_ENTRY_FORMAT`.]
## 7. Handoff to module-decomposition - **Primary input for `module-decomposition`:** `specs/.spec.md` (this file). - **Areas requiring module decomposition:** [Large areas `module-decomposition` will split into modules.] - **Named abstractions (light, for deeper walkthrough):** [Abstractions visible in Golden Example and architecture, no detail.] - **Open risks & validation needs:** [Not closed; must be validated before production.]
```
